title: poa caesia subsp. glauca
intro: Poa caesia subsp. glauca, also known as Blue Fescue, is a subspecies of grass that is native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Poa caesia subsp. glauca include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Blue Fescue can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
